entries.definition:
j–th–th seedling, to transplant; to uproot, to eradicate; body, corpse; impurities. Of this root, اجْتُثَّت ʾijtutththa, occurs once in the Qurʾan.
اُجْتُثَّت ʾujtutththa [pass. of v. VIII] to be uprooted, pulled out (14:26) وَمَثَلُ كَلِمَةٍ خَبِيثَةٍ كَشَجَرَةٍ خَبِيثَةٍ اجْتُثَّتْ مِن فَوْقِ الْأَرْضِ مَا لَهَا مِن قَرَارٍ and the likeness of an evil word is as an evil tree, uprooted from the surface of the earth, with no power to endure.
entries.definition_english:
j–th–th seedling, to transplant; to uproot, to eradicate; body, corpse; impurities. Of this root, اجْتُثَّت ʾijtutththa, occurs once in the Qurʾan.
اُجْتُثَّت ʾujtutththa [pass. of v. VIII] to be uprooted, pulled out (14:26) وَمَثَلُ كَلِمَةٍ خَبِيثَةٍ كَشَجَرَةٍ خَبِيثَةٍ اجْتُثَّتْ مِن فَوْقِ الْأَرْضِ مَا لَهَا مِن قَرَارٍ and the likeness of an evil word is as an evil tree, uprooted from the surface of the earth, with no power to endure.
